label: Critical Algorithms & Surveillance
vocabulary: code as social and political technology

sources.title: Black Mirrors: Reimagining Race, Technology, and Justice

app_prompts: Algorithmic bias case explorer: user picks a domain (hiring, lending, facial recognition, predictive policing) and sees how racial hierarchies get encoded in training data and model design.
Surveillance timeline: user picks a surveillance technology (biometric database, social media monitoring, predictive policing tool) and traces its origins, its stated purpose, and its actual use.
Discriminatory design quiz: user looks at a real interface or system design and identifies the assumptions encoded in it — whose experience was centered, whose was excluded.
Carceral capitalism explainer: user explores how incarceration intersects with economic structures — prison labor, bail bonds, private prisons, the school-to-prison pipeline.

Acts of refusal case study: user reads about a community that resisted surveillance or algorithmic control — facial recognition bans, data strikes, encryption adoption — and traces what worked and why.
